questions.q: Which numbered President of India was Giani Zail Singh?
questions.explanation: Giani Zail Singh served as the seventh President of India.

questions.q: In which year did Giani Zail Singh begin his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: He assumed office as President in 1982.

questions.q: In which year did Giani Zail Singh complete his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: His presidential term concluded in 1987.

questions.q: Giani Zail Singh holds the distinction of being the first member of which community to become President of India?
questions.explanation: He was the first Sikh to hold the office of President.

questions.q: Of which state was Giani Zail Singh earlier Chief Minister?
questions.explanation: He had served as Chief Minister of Punjab.

questions.q: Which Union cabinet portfolio did Giani Zail Singh hold before becoming President?
questions.explanation: He served as Union Home Minister before his presidency.

questions.q: The President's office held by Zail Singh is the head of which?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the constitutional head of state.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did Giani Zail Singh serve as President of India?
questions.explanation: He served from 1982 to 1987, a term of about five years.

questions.q: Which of these offices did Giani Zail Singh hold at the level of a state government?
questions.explanation: Chief Minister of Punjab was his state-level office.

questions.q: The Home Minister portfolio that Zail Singh held is part of which government?
questions.explanation: The Home Minister is part of the Union Council of Ministers.

questions.q: The President of India, the office Zail Singh held, is elected by an electoral college composed of which members?
options: Only members of the Rajya Sabha
Elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: The President is chosen by an electoral college of elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: As President, Giani Zail Singh was the supreme commander of which body?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the supreme commander of the armed forces.

questions.q: Which two earlier offices held by Zail Singh were at the Union and state levels respectively?
options: Union Home Minister and Chief Minister of Punjab
questions.explanation: He was Union Home Minister at the centre and Chief Minister of Punjab at the state level.

questions.q: The normal term of office of the President, which Zail Singh held, is how many years?
questions.explanation: The President of India is elected for a five-year term.

questions.q: Zail Singh's presidency immediately followed that of which President?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Neelam Sanjiva Reddy, the sixth President.

questions.q: Zail Singh's presidency was immediately followed by that of which President?
questions.explanation: R. Venkataraman, the eighth President, succeeded Zail Singh.

questions.q: Punjab, the state Zail Singh led as Chief Minister, lies in which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: Punjab is a state in northern India.

questions.q: The President whom Zail Singh became is a constituent part of which legislative body?
questions.explanation: Parliament consists of the President and the two Houses.

questions.q: The Home Ministry, which Zail Singh headed, is chiefly concerned with which broad subject?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Home Affairs deals with internal security and home affairs.

questions.q: Zail Singh's term as President fell in which decade?
questions.explanation: His 1982 to 1987 term fell within the 1980s.

questions.q: Which sequence correctly orders Zail Singh's offices over time?
options: Chief Minister, then Union Home Minister, then President
President, then Home Minister, then Chief Minister
Home Minister, then President, then Chief Minister
President, then Chief Minister, then Home Minister
questions.explanation: He served as Chief Minister, then as Union Home Minister, before becoming President.

questions.q: Zail Singh's distinction as the first Sikh President relates to which kind of milestone?
options: A community first for the office
questions.explanation: His distinction was being the first member of the Sikh community to hold the office.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, the executive power of the Union, exercisable through the President's office held by Zail Singh, is vested by which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.q: The President's power to grant pardons and reprieves, available to Zail Singh in office, is provided under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 gives the President the power to grant pardons, reprieves and remissions.

questions.q: When granting assent to bills, the President, as Zail Singh was, may also do which of the following with certain bills under Article 111?
options: Return a bill (other than a money bill) for reconsideration
Strike down a law as unconstitutional
questions.explanation: Under Article 111 the President may return a bill, other than a money bill, for reconsideration by Parliament.

questions.q: The office of Governor, the state-level counterpart to the President whom Zail Singh became, is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: Governors of states are appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: The President, the office Zail Singh held, acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 74 provides for a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President.

questions.q: The Home Minister's portfolio, held by Zail Singh, sits within which collective body that aids and advises the President?
questions.explanation: The Home Minister is a member of the Union Council of Ministers.

questions.q: Which statement about the chronology of Zail Singh's career is accurate?
options: He was Union Home Minister before becoming President
He was President before becoming Home Minister
He held both offices at once
questions.explanation: He served as Union Home Minister prior to assuming the presidency.

questions.q: The President's term of office, as held by Zail Singh, runs for five years from which point under the Constitution?
options: The date the President enters upon the office
questions.explanation: The five-year term is counted from the date the President enters upon the office.
